items.title: Epic Fury Has Been an 'Epic Failure' Says Rep. Dean
items.summary: Congresswoman Madeleine Dean, a Pennsylvania Democrat and member of House committees on foreign affairs and appropriations joined Balance of Power to discuss the Iran war diplomacy and the administration’s budget requests. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Today, no one on Earth can say that we are not fighting for Crimea or that we have forgotten about Crimea. Every day proves that Ukraine remembers every part of its land, every one of its people, and is truly fighting to ensure justice for our entire state and every integral part of it. Russia brought its war to Ukraine, starting nowhere other than Crimea, and now, with our actions – medium-range sanctions against the Russian occupier, our long-range sanctions plan, and all other forms of pressure on the aggressor – we are doing everything to force Russia to end the war and restore justice. And it is Crimea that is at the center of this policy of ensuring justice. Russia turned Crimea into a launchpad for aggressive operations against other parts of our country and against other nations. For a very long time, Russia has tried to normalize its war – to make Ukraine, all of Europe, and the world accept that its aggression supposedly cannot be stopped and that justice supposedly cannot be restored. Today, Ukraine is depriving Russia of this launchpad and drawing a line under its attempts to normalize war. And in doing so, we remember that justice for Ukraine also means justice for the Crimean Tatar people – a people forced to endure deportation and decades of oppression, a people who were able to return to their homeland, but whose home the Russians tried to steal again, a people who have preserved their vitality and remain an inseparable part of our society. Today, as we honor the blue banner with the golden tamğa – this proud flag that unites the entire Crimean Tatar people – we affirm that Ukraine is fighting for itself and all its lands, defending justice for itself and all its people – for everyone who calls Ukraine and our Crimea their home. We must stop Russian aggression. We must bring freedom back to all those who are currently in Russian captivity. We must guarantee security and a reliable peace for the entirety of our state and all our people. Qırımtatar Milliy Bayraq künü hayırlı olsun! Glory to Ukraine!
items.summary:
Today, no one on Earth can say that we are not fighting for Crimea or that we have forgotten about Crimea. Every day proves that Ukraine remembers every part of its land, every one of its people, and is truly fighting to ensure justice for our entire state and every integral part of it.

items.title: Brookfield Is in Fight Over Solar Firm Where Cash ‘Evaporated’
items.summary: Joh. Berenberg, Gossler & Co. KG and Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. are battling over the future of GoldenPeaks, an energy company staring at collapse after borrowing $1.5 billion for European solar-power projects.

items.title: Euro Zone Probably Saw First Inflation Slowdown Since Iran War Began
items.summary: The euro zone’s first signs of respite in inflation since the outbreak of the Iran war are forecast for data next week that will reflect the impact of subsiding energy costs.

Russia must get out of Ukraine with its war – we want no war. Ukraine has put forward proposals to our key partners, and Putin’s friends have also heard from us that a meeting is possible and that ending this war is possible. Russia must now take that step toward peace.

items.title: Trump Says Iran Violated Ceasefire With Hormuz Drone Attack
items.summary: US President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating their ceasefire agreement by firing at cargo ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, underscoring how unsettled the situation remains in a waterway critical to global energy flows. Rear Admiral Mark Montgomery (Ret.), former-Destroyer Squadron Commander in the United States Navy and Senior Fellow at the Foundation for Defense of Democracies joined

items.title: Trump accuses Iran of ceasefire breach after Strait of Hormuz attack
items.summary: A Singapore-flagged ship was struck crossing the waterway on Thursday, prompting a large-scale evacuation to be paused.

items.title: Trump Claims Iran Violated Ceasefire With Hormuz Drone Attack
items.summary: US President Donald Trump said Iran firing at cargo ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz violated their ceasefire agreement. In a social media post Friday, Trump said “at least four” one-way attack drones targeted ships in the waterway. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)
